source.title: Calypso Casino first-look map tour (roof to basement walkthrough)
source.notes: IMPORTANT: this is NOT a structured attack setup guide — it is a casual first-look walkthrough of the map (spawns on the roof, breaks soft walls with a hammer/shotgun while narrating layout and room names) recorded before the creator had played real rounds on it. He explicitly says a proper setup-guide video with attack strategies will come later once he has more experience on the map. As a result there is very little breach-plan, drone-path, or operator-recommendation content — most of what's extractable is layout/connectivity, external breach locations, rappel points, and drone vent locations, which is what this file focuses on. There are almost no named operators anywhere in this transcript (only Sledge is mentioned, as the operator the creator is running for the tour, not as a strategic recommendation) so operatorRecommendations is sparse/empty by design, not omission. Room names are the creator's own best-guess callouts, several of which he explicitly says he's unsure of or contradicts himself on mid-video (e.g. 'gambling' vs 'betting', 'humidor' vs 'cigar shop'). Treat site boundaries here as inferred from the Defense-side companion video (Calypso_Defense_qf3AthSTIDQ.txt), since this attack tour does not clearly declare bomb sites itself.

hatches.location: Humidor / connector room near the aquarium
hatches.note: Small square connector room off the aquarium area has a hatch in it.

gadgetPlacements.note: Attackers can spawn on the roof on the pool side; lots of roof space up there with a sub-roof level above the main roof.

Several rappel-down points from the roof, including one that goes all the way down into Main Lobby, and one reachable only via a 'rappel swift' move down the side to a breach that's otherwise very hard to reach.

gadgetPlacements.location: Barista/Kitchen soft wall separating Kitchen from Barista
gadgetPlacements.note: Soft wall here connects Kitchen straight into Barista.

gadgetPlacements.location: Barista window facing outside (Tequila Bar side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Soft wall/window here looks into the back of the Tequila Bar; flagged as a notable connector between outside and the Barista interior.

gadgetPlacements.location: Jukebox / speakers near South Mezzanine and Main Lobby
gadgetPlacements.note: Speakers here can be shot out for a sound cue removal, same as noted on defense.

generalTips.note: The whole top floor forms a ring with no middle: Barista connects to Kitchen, to Humidor/Cigar, to Fish Stairs, and back around — you cannot cut straight across the top of Main Lobby, only go around the outside or down/up through it.

There's a lot of soft-wall density in this area (Kitchen/Cigar/Humidor cluster) — creator notes it feels intricate with many breakable walls, though acknowledges he may be misjudging this on a first look.

hatches.note: A hatch to drop down is present near the server/vault ventilation area.

gadgetPlacements.note: External breach here — drones can be sent in through it from outside before attackers commit.
Two soft walls in this room, both breakable, connecting further into CCTV.
Soft wall connection; creator ran out of hammer charges here and had to shotgun through.
This wall opens directly to outside the building, with a rappel drop right there — flagged as a distinctive, possibly reinforced-in-practice wall worth knowing about for external entry.
Ducts present in this area — noted but not fully explored on this pass.
Soft wall leads to an unfinished/'secret area' looking floor with a ladder, which itself connects back outside — effectively a second, less obvious external access point into the Vault/Server area.

generalTips.note: This whole basement area (Managers, Weapon Cache, CCTV, Server, Vault Checkpoint) has more external breach points than any other part of the map found on this pass, including at least two ways to enter from completely outside the building.

hatches.note: Hatch present on the connector between Bathroom and the Blackjack/Poker/Roulette cluster.

gadgetPlacements.note: Blackjack has an external soft-wall breach (does not connect Poker/Blackjack directly — that connecting wall is NOT breakable). The external breach gives an angle described as similar to the outside angle on Consulate... [note: creator actually compares it to 'the angle on layer outside operational'] — worth scouting before committing, attackers can be shot rappelling on it.

Secret is a dead-end room with no interior route in — the only way in is vaulting through the window, since the soft wall there realistically wouldn't be breached (creator's in-game observation, not a real strategic recommendation).
Registration is another dead-end-style room with a soft wall leading outside.

generalTips.note: Poker, Blackjack, Roulette, and Betting are the four 'gambling rooms' clustered together — creator flags Blackjack/Poker as the room pairing most likely to be an actual bomb site given the layout (consistent with the companion Defense video, which confirms this).
Blackjack/Poker external breach angle is highlighted as a notable defender sightline to be cautious of when rappelling or pushing that side.

hatches.location: Bar, near the stairs down to Managers
hatches.note: Hatch present near the bar stairwell leading down toward the basement Managers area.

gadgetPlacements.note: A drone-accessible vent was found here that drops a drone down into the map from above — flagged as a very hard-to-spot pre-placed drone spot for attackers to exploit, and a correspondingly hard angle for defenders to watch. Creator suggests a Keeper-style barrier could shut it down, though notes that may be an inefficient use of the gadget.
Soft wall connector leading to another set of stairs and back around into Bar/Janitor.
Connects back around to the roof rappel-down point the creator used at the start of the tour, confirming this loop connects all the way up to the roof.

generalTips.note: This whole side of the map (Bar/Betting/Wine Stock/Janitor/Tequila Stairs) is described as noticeably bigger than the opposite (Blackjack/Poker/Secret/Registration) side, with more depth and rotational options.
Tequila Stairs connects up to the Tequila Bar seen from outside near the top-floor Barista window — confirms a vertical link between this ground-floor area and the top floor.
